title: Level !
wikitext:
{{Collapse|num=1|initial=

[[File:Level!iconbycringeithinkthankyoucringeI.png|200px|center]]

You've been wandering aimlessly in this seemingly endless parking lot for what feels like an eternity. Hunger gnaws at your insides, and fear courses through your veins. Every shadow, every creaking sound sets your heart racing, as you're constantly on high alert, dreading the sudden appearance of those [[Entity 3|nightmarish grinning faces]] and [[Entity 8|the bloodthirsty hounds]]. Suddenly, a glimmer catches your eye in the distance. There, a door materializes. The "EXIT" sign above it shines brightly, like a beacon of hope in this sea of dread. Your eyes widen, and without a second thought, you think to yourself, "That must be the way out!" Ignoring the soreness in your legs and the fatigue weighing you down, you lift your heavy, leaden feet and start sprinting towards it. As you draw nearer, a creeping sense of foreboding washes over you. Doubts start to creep into your mind. Could this be yet another trap? Another gateway to a different level of this terrifying, nightmarish realm? You hesitate, your steps slowing down as you try to make sense of the situation. Just as you're lost in thought, the lights flicker once, twice, and then go out completely. The world around you plunges into a darkness so thick that it's almost palpable. In this pitch-black void, you hear a deep, guttural growl that sends shivers down your spine. Then, out of the darkness, several [[Entity 3|glowing faces]] materialize. Their eyes, like two burning coals, are fixed on you, and they're steadily approaching. Panic sets in. Your heart pounds in your chest so hard that you can hear it in your ears. With no other options in sight, you have no choice but to reach out your hand and push open the door.


|final=|body=


{{Alt Class|ext=4|env=5|ent=5|no=!}}

Level ! is the most dangerous anomalous level in the Backrooms, and nobody should deliberately enter it for any purpose, otherwise you will do so at your own risk. It is known by various names, such as Run for your life!, Level Run, or simply Run. Prior to the introduction of CBNS, it was also known as Area-847. {{Blurimage|file=Level!Entrance.jpeg|caption=An entrance to Level !.|width=350px}} Entry
Generally speaking, there are two main ways to enter Level !. The first way is to pass through an exit door that is incompatible with its surroundings. These doors are believed to appear randomly in any level, and their positions keep changing, making it difficult for the M.E.G. to block them. Once you enter this door, it will close immediately. When the wanderer turns back, they will find a huge red exclamation mark printed on the door. At this point, they are already in Level !. The second way usually involves a series of elements related to exclamation marks. For example, noclipping into objects related to exclamation marks may lead to entering this level. Additionally, walking into an elevator with an exclamation mark printed on it may result in the door opening to reveal that the wanderer has entered Level !. In addition, there are some other ways that may lead to entering Level !. Having a certain thought in Phenomenon 354 will definitely cause the wanderer to enter this level. Noclipping through the floor in Level 2 also has a chance of enabling the wanderer to enter this level. When multiple people enter the level at the same time, they will be forced to separate.
Exclamation Marks
It is worth noting that Level ! was previously known as Area - 847. The entry reports of this level indicate that before Area - 847 was renamed Level !, no one had entered this level through elements related to the exclamation mark. Any wanderers who entered this level by going through the exit door also claimed that they did not see the red exclamation mark behind the door. This phenomenon is extremely perplexing. It seems to imply that the Backrooms truly have an inevitable connection with human thoughts. It is regarded as an effective refutation of The Random Theory, demonstrating that the Backrooms are not as disorderly as many people imagine, and may even operate in a certain specific way.
- [[Alex Matthews]], M.E.G.



{{Collapse|num=2|initial=

The door slammed shut with a deafening bang, sending a shockwave of fear through your body. You whipped around in a panic, your heart pounding in your chest, and saw a huge, menacing exclamation mark emblazoned on the door. In that split second, a cold realization washed over you, and you understood the dire situation you were in. You lunged forward, desperately trying to wrench the door open and make your getaway, but it was as if it had been sealed shut by some invisible force. No matter how hard you pulled or pushed, it wouldn't budge an inch. With a sinking feeling in your stomach, you took a step back and surveyed your surroundings. This was a hospital room, but it was bathed in an eerie, blood-red glow that made the air feel thick and oppressive. The intense red light stabbed at your eyes, making them water and ache with fatigue. On the hospital bed lay a neatly folded bedsheet, its crisp edges contrasting sharply with the chaotic fear that filled the room. The silence was deafening, broken only by the rapid thudding of your own heart. In a last-ditch effort to find a way out, you fumbled for your Major Navigation System and switched it on, your hands shaking with anticipation. But as the screen flickered to life, a huge red exclamation mark stared back at you, dashing your final hopes of a quick escape. In front of you, two doors stood, like silent sentinels guarding your fate. One of them bore an exit sign, a beacon of hope in this hellish place, while the other was chained shut, its heavy links glinting menacingly in the red light. Suddenly, a deafening crash shattered the silence, coming from the door that was chained shut. Your blood ran cold as you realized that there was something on the other side, something that was desperately trying to break free and unleash its fury upon you. It could either tear you apart limb from limb or consume you whole. Bang! Another thunderous sound echoed through the room, and you could see the door shudder under the force of the impact. They were almost through. Panic surged through you like a wildfire, consuming every thought and leaving only one imperative: run. Get out of this nightmare before it was too late. Without a moment's hesitation, you bolted towards the exit door, your feet pounding on the cold, hard floor.


|final= |body= Arrival
{{Blurimage|file=HospitalRoom!I.png|caption=A sketch of the room. Any attempt to photograph this room will result in a large red exclamation mark.|width=350px}} When wanderers reach this level, they will find themselves in a room illuminated by red light. The room is furnished in a very simple manner, with only some basic items. In the center of the room stands a hospital bed, on which several quilts are neatly folded. Next to the bed is a bedside table for placing daily items, and on one side of the bed, there is a curtain that can be used for screening. In the corner of the room, there is a medicine cabinet for storing medications. Wanderers can sometimes find useful supplies such as almond water, energy bars, etc. They are often placed in the corners of bedside tables or medicine cabinets, and other places. These supplies will give the wanderer energy to prepare for the rest of the run. Apart from the door through which the wanderer entered (if the wanderer entered this level through the exit door), there are two other doors in the room, facing each other. One of the doors is tightly chained shut. Although it is difficult to see what is behind the door from the outside, the sound of countless entities trying to break through the door can be heard from the other side. The other door leads to a hallway, which is the only way for the wanderers. It should be noted that, in extremely rare cases, instead of appearing in the aforementioned room, wanderers will directly enter the hallway upon arriving at this level. The triggering mechanism of this special situation remains unclear.
Survival Guide #1:
Gather supplies as fast as you can.

If you happen to find yourself in Level !, the initial impulse might be overwhelming. You'll likely think about making a beeline for the exit door without a moment's hesitation and then pushing yourself to complete a grueling 10 - kilometer long - distance run as fast as your legs can carry you. It's a natural reaction, a fight - or - flight response kicking in, with the urgency to escape this strange and potentially dangerous realm. But hold on a second! Our team of seasoned experts have carefully evaluated the situation. They firmly believe that investing a mere 5 seconds in gathering supplies at this crucial moment is a far more strategic and beneficial choice. Now, you might be thinking, "What difference can 5 seconds possibly make?" Well, these 5 seconds, on their own, won't catapult you light - years ahead of the lurking entities in this level. They won't create an insurmountable gap between you and the potential threats. However, the supplies you manage to acquire within this brief window of time most certainly will. In fact, as you progress deeper into the long - distance run, when your legs start to feel like lead, your breath becomes ragged, and every step is a Herculean effort, these supplies will prove to be an invaluable asset. They can transform from simple items into a wellspring of motivation, the very thing that keeps you putting one foot in front of the other when your body is screaming at you to stop. But here's a word of caution: resist the urge to dip into these supplies during the early stages of the run. Think of them as your secret weapons, reserved for when the going gets really tough. However, it's not just about amassing as many supplies as you can. You must also exercise restraint. Carrying an excessive amount of supplies is like strapping weights to your ankles. It will slow you down, sap your energy, and ultimately, might even make it impossible for you to keep running at a sustainable pace. So, find that delicate balance. Another key point to remember is time management. Don't waste precious minutes scouring every nook and cranny in search of supplies. If, as you quickly scan the area around you, your eyes don't land on any visible sources of sustenance or useful items, it's a clear sign to cut your losses. Move on without hesitation, because in this high - stakes situation, every second counts. - Major Explorer Group





Explore, create a government, be human.









{{Collapse|num=3|initial=

Run for your life! In the dim space, the dead silence was instantly torn apart by a deafening alarm. The sharp sound reverberated back and forth in the enclosed space, as if it was about to pierce through one's eardrums. You looked back in horror. In the darkness, countless shadowy figures were surging, and then, spine-chilling roars came crashing in, filled with endless greed and brutality, as if they were going to devour you alive. "Run, run for your life!" A voice in your heart was screaming frantically. You took off running, the soles of your shoes rubbing violently against the ground, making a harsh sound. At this moment, a fine but oppressive vibration came from above your head. It was the Deathmoths! Every flap of their huge wings seemed to strike heavily on your heart. Several Hounds also joined this thrilling hunt. Their fierce barks rose and fell, sharp and menacing, each sound hitting the depths of your fear. You had no idea how many terrifying entities were hiding behind you. There seemed to be endless danger lurking in the darkness. What was even more desperate was that the exit sign in front, which was supposed to guide you to escape, kept repeating in your sight. Every time you saw that familiar sign, the hope that ignited in your heart would quickly be extinguished by the cold despair. You didn't know if this desperate run would ever come to an end, how much longer you could hold on, or if you would be caught by those terrifying entities the next second. But for now, in this desperate situation, running was the only option. Faster.


|final= |body= Hallway
{{Blurimage|file=level!halfwayI.png|caption=An image of the hallway. Due to the danger of the level, the photographer is considered to be [[Entity 1|The Cameraman]]|width=350px}} After leaving the room, wanderers will enter a hallway that is ten kilometers long. The appearance of this hallway is similar to that of a modern hospital hallway. There are exit signs installed on the ceiling, emitting a deep red glow, which is considered to be the only light source within the level. These exit signs are installed in almost random positions, resulting in some areas being overly bright while some are plunged into complete darkness. For this reason, flashlights are regarded as highly practical supplies, which can effectively help wanderers avoid obstacles in dark areas. Obstacles are randomly placed within the level and may slide around randomly, causing great inconvenience to the wanderers' movement. Large obstacles (such as chairs, beds, etc.) may block the wanderers' progress when running forward, while smaller obstacles (such as toy cars, boxes, etc.) are even more lethal, they are difficult to spot, yet can cause wanderers to trip and waste a great deal of time. An alarm of unknown origin is continuously playing within the level. The loudness is extremely high, and it has been proven to be harmful to the mental state of wanderers. Wanderers may become panicked due to this alarm and are unable to think rationally, ultimately leading to being caught by entities. Some people believe that this alarm actually exists in the wanderers' minds rather than within the level, but this view has not been confirmed. On both sides of the Hallway are doors made of an unknown metal, which are indestructible. Most of these doors cannot be opened for unknown reasons. Some of the doors that can be opened may lead to a room. Although there may be supplies in the room, once a Wanderer enters, it is almost impossible for them to get out again. In addition, there may be other entities inside these doors. For these reasons, Wanderers should not attempt to open the doors on either side of the Hallway. When the entities break through the door, they will start chasing the Wanderers. The entities here are complex and diverse, encompassing almost all of the entities in the Backrooms, including Deathmoths, Smilers, Partygoers, Aranea Membri, Skin-Stealers, etc. Strangely, the speeds of these entities are nearly the same in this level.
Survival Guide #2:
They are still near!

Persevering in Level ! is hard, especially when you run 5~6 km, the runner will feel exhausted, sore, and likely to give up. At this point, the entity's roar can no longer be heard, and only the shrill sirens remain throughout the level. If you look back, you'll see that there's nothing behind you. Is the entity gone? Maybe you've escaped. But hey! It's just a little trick on the level, the entities haven't disappeared, it's just that they're far away from you, and you can't see them for a while. In this case, you should never stop to rest under any circumstances. Keep running! Once you start to rest, once the entity catches up, you will most likely not be able to continue running. Experts have shown that running for a while and resting for a while takes a lot more energy than running all the time. Always remember: They are still near, don't stop! - Major Explorer Group





Explore, create a government, be human.









Unusual events
If you're unlucky enough, you may encounter some unusual events. These events occur rarely but all pose threats to the wanderers' progress. |-| Explosion= 1 {{Collapse|num=Fire|initial=

Something isn't right. At the very far end of the seemingly endless passage, an exit sign flickered weakly. It wasn't just a normal, intermittent blink; it was a spasmodic, erratic twitching, accompanied by a persistent, high - pitched hissing sound. You squinted, trying to make out the details in the gloom. It was as if the sign was struggling, as if it was on the verge of giving up its long - standing duty of guiding lost souls to safety. Entities behind you does not allow you to think further about what will happen to it, and you can only pray that nothing terrible will happen. However, the fates seemed to have other plans. In an instant, a deafening explosion shattered the eerie silence. The sound was so powerful that it felt like it was rattling your very bones. Simultaneously, a blinding flash of white light erupted from the direction of the exit sign, momentarily engulfing everything in its path. When your vision finally cleared, you saw that the once - familiar sign was now nothing but a smoking ruin. The explosion had a catastrophic domino effect. Flames, hungry and fierce, quickly spread, consuming every visible obstacle in their way. The old, tattered sheets that were haphazardly strewn about caught fire immediately, and within seconds, they were ablaze, forming a wall of searing heat that blocked your last possible escape route. The flames danced and twisted in a grotesque manner, their orange - red glow casting an otherworldly light on the scene. It was as if they were alive, mocking your desperate situation. The crackling sound of the burning seemed to be a cruel laugh, a reminder that your hopes of a quick and easy escape were now nothing but ashes. As you stared at the inferno in front of you, your mind raced. There seemed to be no other choice. The only way out of this nightmarish situation was to summon all your courage and force your way through that wall of fire. With a deep breath, you closed your eyes.


|final= |body=

{{Blurimage|file=fire🔥🔥🔥I.jpeg|align=center|caption=Level ! on fire, photographer had to run across the fire. He eventually escaped from level !.|width=350px}}



In Level !, the stability of exit signs differs from what is expected, presenting certain abnormal characteristics. Through observation, the exit signs in this area are not as stable and reliable as in normal environments. Instead, they may explode suddenly in rare cases. Once an explosion occurs, it is highly likely to trigger a series of chain reactions. The most common consequence is that various items in the vicinity will be ignited instantly, creating a dangerous sea of fire. When such a situation occurs, if the wanderers in the area want to move forward, they have to pass through the blazing firewall. However, this process is fraught with risks. Wanderers are highly likely to suffer severe burns, with their skin and clothing potentially damaged. At the same time, in the panic of crossing or due to the obstruction of vision caused by the fire, wanderers may also trip over surrounding debris, thus falling into a more dangerous situation and even endangering their lives. It is worth noting that before the explosion of the exit sign, there are usually some detectable signs. For example, the sign will flicker irregularly, with its frequency and rhythm showing no pattern. It will also emit a harsh sound, which is sharp and continuous and can easily attract the attention of wanderers. Some signs will also have an excessively high brightness, far exceeding the normal working level and even possibly reaching a dazzling degree. When traveling in Level !, wanderers must always pay attention to these abnormal signs to prepare in advance. Regarding the reason why these exit sign explosions only occur in front of the wanderers, there is currently no conclusive answer, and there are various speculations. Although some people believe that this may be merely caused by an ordinary circuit failure, given the special nature of Level !'s environment, this view still needs further verification. }} |-| Entities= The doors on both sides are not always locked; they can sometimes be opened by wanderers. Behind the doors may lie a room, or perhaps [[The Void|nothing at all]]. Incidents of both doors being opened have occurred extremely rarely, with only three or four unconfirmed reports claiming to have encountered this situation to date. Even if true, it is not recommended for wanderers to attempt opening both doors, as you gain nothing from it while wasting time. More commonly, entities burst through the doors on both sides. These suddenly appearing entities usually move much slower than the entities that chase after wanderers. Precisely because of this, they mostly serve to intimidate the wanderers. Under no circumstances should one actively open the unlocked doors on both sides, as there may be countless entities lurking there. Even if there aren't any, opening these doors will never bring any benefits to the wanderers - there are usually no supplies there. In addition, entities sometimes emerge from the ventilation ducts above the hallways. This applies to smaller-sized entities or those that can change their shapes, such as Hounds, Smilers, and so on.
Survival Guide #3:
Who's that?

If entities come out of doors or ventilation ducts, you'll most likely need to find a way to get rid of them. It's not easy. In this regard, the best thing to do is to focus on: Who is that? Each entity will have its own way of responding. As long as you analyze the characteristics of the entity, you can find a suitable way to deal with it. Smilers are one of the most terrifying entities in the Backrooms, and the greatest threat a person can encounter in Level !. You can stop them from leaving the door by shining a flashlight on it. If you get too far away, they will lose interest in you. If the Smilers are after you, you can shoot down the exit signs, which can help you get rid of them with the flames that ignite. Deathmoths, my goodness, they're a bunch of flying demons! These terrifying creatures will attack you from above, but luckily the ceiling of the level is not high. However, these creatures don't seem to be very intelligent, and you can get their attention by throwing things like bottles - sometimes they're too stupid to distinguish between human objects. Hounds are a type of dog – only more intelligent than the average dog. Contrary to Smilers, they love light, and you can disorient them by shooting at the lighting within the level. These are the three examples we have given, the entities that pop up here also include Skin-stealers, Wretches, etc., read their documentation to understand them, all entities have weaknesses - know them and use them, and you can easily deal with those creatures! - Major Explorer Group





Explore, create a government, be human.









|-| Collapse= The environment of the level doesn't seem to be that stable. Sometimes, the environment of the level can cause great trouble to the wanderers. When you notice cracks appearing on the ground, try your best not to step on that part of the ground - it may collapse at any moment. Regarding those collapsed grounds and the wanderers who fall along with them, we never know where they end up. The walls and the ceiling may also collapse. Before the collapse, wanderers can usually observe obvious cracks as well. Wanderers should try their best to avoid being hit by the walls and the ceiling, because this may waste a great deal of time. When the ceiling falls, it has been observed that power lines may hang from above. These wires pose a great danger to wanderers, it may trip over wanderers, or electrocute them. It is unclear whether these wires provide power for the exit markings. In addition, if there is an exit sign on the collapsed ceiling, it may explode.
What's Behind That Wall?
...In this part, I've talked a lot about the theories of CBNS and the study of levels, and here I would like to ask you a question: What's behind that wall? As mentioned earlier, the scientists in the Backrooms have confirmed that the levels are arranged like the floors of a building. Generally speaking, it is possible that behind the wall of a building will be a community with more buildings, or something else. So, what's going to be behind the walls of the Backrooms? Some people think it's [[The Void]], or the [[Uncharted Territory]]. The existence of Level ! seems to give its own answer. An unnamed wanderer claimed to have seen the world behind the wall during a collapse event - there was only another hallway, and another human running just like him. You can think of it as a little story, after all, hallucinations happen from time to time in Level !. But I still want you to think about the question – what's behind that wall? Right, moving to a brief review of...
- A transcript of a lecture by Dr.Stapel at Base Alpha.
|-| Fake Exit= 1 {{Collapse|num=fakeexit|initial=

You have been running for I don't know how long. You are extremely exhausted, and the hope of finding an exit has reached its peak. Just then, a door appears in the distance. There is a sign on that door. You take a closer look and find that it actually says "EXIT". It is shining with a brilliant red light, as if congratulating you on finally escaping from this level. Is that the exit? You can finally get away from this terrifying place, return to your outpost, and see your lovely colleagues and superiors. You can hug them and tell them about your thrilling experience. The news of your escape from this red corridor will spread to every corner of the Backrooms, and people will regard you as a legend - a legend who has escaped from the most dangerous, mysterious and strange place in the Backrooms... Wait a moment. Just as you grasp the handle of the exit and are about to rush in, you stop. You look around and see another corridor on the right side. The previous joy gives way to reason - could this be a trap? You carefully push open the door - it's just an empty room. The entities are about to catch up. At this critical moment, you run to the right. That was close. You almost got deceived by this level again.


|final=|body= In Level !, false exits are everywhere. Bends appear every few kilometers in the corridor, which will give wanderers the illusion that they have reached the exit. If you see an exit door, but at the same time notice a bend next to the door, do not go directly to that door as it is a false exit. The exit sign for false exports is usually and a little darker. False exits usually lead to an empty room, and will cause wanderers to be caught by entities. In other cases, these exits may also cause wanderers to re-enter Level !. However, there are rumors that some special exit doors lead to some anomalous level, but this is unproven, and it clearly does not deserve to be confirmed. }}

{{Collapse|num=5365|initial=

In the crimson hallway, a flash of blue light seems rather uncommon. At the very first moment you laid eyes on that blue glow, a spark of hope ignited within you. Your mind instinctively jumped to the conclusion that it could be the long - awaited exit, the gateway to freedom from this torturous realm. But just as quickly as that hope flared up, a wave of caution washed over you. Memories of all the cruel tricks and traps this malevolent level had already subjected you to came flooding back. You remembered the countless times you had been lured into false hopes, only to be plunged deeper into despair. Now, all you could do was pray with all your might that this blue light wasn't just another elaborate trap, a sinister ploy to ensnare you further and prolong your suffering. As you continued to stumble forward, the blue light grew steadily brighter, its intensity increasing with every passing second. At the same time, the menacing growl of some unknown entity was approaching from behind. The sound seemed to reverberate through your very bones, sending shivers down your spine. You could feel the hot, fetid breath of the creature on the back of your neck, and you were certain that at any moment, its sharp claws or teeth would sink into your flesh. Your heart was pounding so hard in your chest that it felt like it was about to burst. You wanted to run faster, to put as much distance as possible between yourself and the approaching danger, but your body, already pushed to its absolute limit, refused to cooperate. Your muscles burned with fatigue, and your legs threatened to give out at any moment. Finally, after what felt like an eternity of struggling, you were close enough to make out the source of the blue light clearly. It was a door, a simple yet imposing blue door. The sight of it filled you with a mix of relief and trepidation. Was this really the exit? Could it be the end of your ordeal? There was no way to be sure, but you knew that you had no other choice. With a final burst of courage, you held your breath, closed your eyes, and threw yourself headfirst into the blue door. For a brief moment, everything seemed to go black. You felt a strange sensation of weightlessness, as if you were floating through some void. And then, as suddenly as it had all started, you felt solid ground beneath your feet once again. When you opened your eyes, you found yourself in a blue - lit hallway. The air here was clean and fresh, a stark contrast to the putrid atmosphere of the hallway you had just escaped from. Your eyes scanned the hallway, and soon landed on a sign on the wall. It bore a symbol of a pair of wings and a sun, a symbol that you instantly recognized as the mark of M.E.G. A wave of overwhelming relief washed over you, and you sank to your knees, thanking whatever forces had finally shown you mercy.
WELCOME
to the safe zone.

Please take a break before you continue running.



|final= |body=
Safe Zone
{{Blurimage|file=SafezoneinI.png|caption=An image of the safe zone, taken by user TheOneWhoLived on Backchat.|width=350px}} The Safe Zone is an area within Level ! where one can take a rest, no entity is likely to enter here. It can be said that this area is extremely rare, and not all wanderers can come across it. In the confirmed reports, only about 6% of them mention this area. The Safe Zone is a place similar to the exterior area of Level !, but unlike the Corridor, it is predominantly blue and significantly more well-lit. Doors on both sides can be opened, and behind the doors is a room. The furnishings in the room include a shelf and a bed. Wanderers can find items including almond water, energy bars, and more in these places. If you want to leave the safe zone, just go out through the door at the end of the safe zone. After exiting the door of the safe zone, the entity will enter through the entrance of the safe zone and continue to chase the wanderer.


Survival Guide #4:
Take your time.

If you come across a blue space in Level !, congratulations! You're in the safe zone. Entities will not enter here. There are always some wanderers who are in a hurry to finish the run, so they rest in the safety zone for ten minutes and then leave in a hurry – we say it doesn't have to be. You should at least rest in the safe zone for a few hours or a day, which will greatly increase your chances of survival. On the contrary, if you only rest for ten minutes, your chances of surviving will be reduced. This doesn't mean you can rest in the safe zone forever, where food is limited. You take a maximum of two or three days off, at which point most of the food will be consumed. If you stay here any longer, there's a good chance your energy won't be able to support you anymore. Therefore, how long to rest in the safe zone and how to rest needs to be carefully considered, otherwise you are likely to reduce your chances of survival. Time is of the essence. - Major Explorer Group





Explore, create a government, be human.









{{Collapse|num=5623948|initial=

Another exit. You're tired of one fake exit after another, your tired body is no longer able to think, and there's only one thought left in your brain: run. However, before that, you thought that maybe this level didn't have any exits at all, and it was just fun to play tricks on you. So what's the point of your run? But in the end you make up your mind that you'd better keep running until you're completely paralyzed, until you're dizzy from exhaustion, until you're finally sure you're never going to be able to escape here. The exit is still ten meters away, and you can't hear the entities roars anymore, but you're sure they're still in pursuit. Five meters. The shrill sirens will paralyze your brain, and the monotonous red color will almost make you lose your vision. Two meters, you can't hold on anymore, you're going to die here. If this exit in front of me isn't the real one, then I'll give up, your last thought. There are no turns on either side. This is the true exit. Your adrenaline rushes, caution and joy rush through you, you clench your grip and open the door, praying for what's behind it. You close your eyes so nervously, then open them again and what you see is like a warehouse—a place you know all too well, a resting area you've dreamed of. Entities behind you catches up, you quickly run out of the hallway, closing the door tightly. This is the end of this long nightmare.


|final=|body= The Exit
After running for approximately 10 km, the wanderer will reach the exit of Level !. If the wanderer looks to both sides and there are no other hallways, this is a true exit door. Exiting through this door usually means escaping Level !. It is important to note that after exiting the level, it is important to close and mark the exit door, as it has been documented that the exit door has been converted into an entrance door. Once a wanderer leaves Level !, he will not re-enter. If he attempts to enter an entrance door to a level, he will only reach another location in the level he was in. In the known reports, wanderers who leave Level ! mostly arrive at safer levels, which means that the threat index of these levels is usually Class Habitable, Class 0, and Class 1, with the most reached levels being Level 1 and Level 4. However, in very, very rare cases, an exit door may allow you to re-enter Level !. In this case, only a very small number of wanderers have escaped the level for the second time.
Survival Guide #4:
Stay calm.

Congratulations, you have escaped this horrible hell! The good news is that you won't be experiencing this nightmare again. You can have some ecstasy, because you have done what most wanderers can't. But don't let the joy get you carried away – always be calm in this part of the Backrooms. Don't take a break immediately after escaping the red corridor, keep walking or running slowly, there is a reason for this. First of all, if you lie down or sit down immediately after the human body's physical strength has reached its limit, it is likely to cause sudden death - you must not want to die after just escaping death. Secondly, it is also possible for wanderers to arrive at the place where the entity exists after leaving the level, so try to run to the nearest outpost to ensure absolute safety. There are very few wanderers who enter Level !, let alone even fewer who succeed in escaping. And this scarlet promenade cannot be accessed a second time. So to this day, we still don't know much about it. If any wanderers escape there, head to the nearby M.E.G. base, where we will be rewarded, and you will help more struggling souls. - Major Explorer Group





Explore, create a government, be human.









Discovery
{{Blurimage|file=19thcenturyrunforyourlife.jpg|align=center|caption=Photo E.|width=500px}}



The official record of Level ! comes from a photograph named Photo E. This photograph was found in Level 52 and is believed to have been taken in the 19th century. Exactly how this photograph was shot is unknown, as 19th-century cameras are noticeably bulky and difficult to use. As a result, more people believe that the photographer of this photo is [[Entity 1|The Cameraman]]. Is this really the case? Also in Level 52, one of the members of the Kauer discovers a journal from a man named Jacques Dubois. If the diary is true, then Jacques Dubois is supposed to be the person who took Photo E. However, due to its age, most of the handwriting in the diary is illegible, and we still have no way of knowing how he took Photo E. Some M.E.G. believe that Level may have once been safe, but the ancient texts from The Lost deny this idea. Others believe that the photograph was taken in Level 16, Level 14, or any other level similar to Level ! {{Blurimage|file=N.C.B.I.png|caption=Noah C. Boyle.|width=350px}} The earliest record of Level ! from the M.E.G., comes from Noah C. Boyle. The member accidentally entered the level while collecting information about a god, and was recorded in audio as he was on a mission. Noah, according to information that has been made public, has died in Level !. However, it is believed that he is actually alive and secretly working for a group.










{{Collapse|num=9949|initial=

It's been a few months since you escaped that nightmare. Over the past few months, the M.E.G. and many other groups have invited you to give a talk to their outpost about how you survived Level !, to encourage the people of the Backrooms to survive. Backrooms Today wants to interview you, as well as some of the other survivors of Level !. In order to get a few bottles of almond water or other supplies, you agreed to their request. Recently, the M.E.G. invited you to listen to a lecture by an expert who studies Level !. And make some comments. It's just another talk, encouraging other people to get paid jobs, and you think so, when you were invited to Base Alpha, expecting to see some kids looking at me with expectant eyes. But when I arrived at the agreed room, it was nothing more than a group of researchers. "Ah, welcome, Wanderer Representative. I'm Dr. Hertz, and I'm the keynote speaker today. ” He turns to the crowd in the room, them seem to be looking at you with a strange look. Dr. Hertz enthusiastically explains: "This is an experienced explorer who has successfully escaped Level !. I said, it is better to see than to hear, and his understanding of this space must be much deeper than those of us who read reports and write papers in the office every day. ” You nodded in confusion, now you don't know any of the theories of the level you've fled, let alone any deep insights. In confusion, you find an empty seat to sit down. "So, I'd like to start by talking about the progress I've made so far about Level !......"


|final= |body= Theories
|-| The Salvation Theory= No matter where you are in the Backrooms, whether it's a level, an object, or a phenomenon, there is always this group of devout believers who tend to use religion to explain everything. Salvation is a theory that is widely circulated among wanderers. This theory is especially popular at a time when human perception of the Backrooms is still vague. This theory holds that anyone who enters the Level is guilty and is punished. Those who flee for their lives are washing away their sins and redeeming their souls. As for those who die in it and will never be able to leave—they have sinned so much that they will never be forgiven by the Lord. As ridiculous as it sounds, creationism is not necessarily wrong, as we have already seen some of the gods of the Backrooms. If the religious element of this theory is discarded, it can become a reasonable scientific conjecture.


Salvation
Those of their people who returned from the expedition said that they had met the most terrible thing in the world. A crimson hallway is filled with the unspeakable. This is the curse of the Lord against sinners, and those who step in should run as hard as they can until their souls are baptized. Only those who have been forgiven by the Lord can leave this place. But if they are the most vicious, they will be trapped in that land for eternity, and there will be no salvation.
- "θέση (Places)", Found in: [[Level 906|The Omnia Athenaeum]]
|-| Theory of Grave Sin=


{{Blurimage|file=IprotestI.png|align=center|caption=A parade event initiated by supporters of this theory took place at Level 403.|width=350px}}


Perhaps due to the mental abnormality caused by too many encounters, there are already a bit too many conspiracy theorists in the Backrooms. The Theory of Grave Sin is significantly too unsubstantiated and illogical compared to other theories, but due to its widespread belief among wanderers, an introduction to it is still necessary. Theory of Grave Sin proposes that the Backrooms are a human testing ground created by a government to screen out humans with the best physical abilities and train them to be excellent agents or super soldiers. Entities in each level are used to screen out those with relatively good physical and intellectual abilities, and those who are favored will enter the level for further physical selection. Don't anger the proponents of the theory, they are all insane. |-| Extraterrestrial Entertainment= It has already been mentioned that there is no shortage of conspiracy theorists in the Backrooms, and there is certainly more than one theory of these conspiracy theorists. Due to the supernatural nature of the Backrooms, many believe that the Backrooms were actually built by non-humans – that is, aliens. Some of them believe that the aliens built the Backrooms and selected humans to enter the Backrooms as circus clowns for their viewing and entertainment. For the more violent, evil aliens, Level has become a great place for them to entertain. It is even believed that the aliens will bet on which humans can escape from the level as a gambling game. These theories are described in detail in the novel "Above all" by Leica Jr. |-| The Kauer Thesis= The Kauer Theory believes that Level represents a total fear of chasing in the Backrooms. In fact, no matter how lucky they are, there is a high probability that they have been chased by entities in the Backrooms. Due to the near-ubiquity of entities, some wanderers have made it to outposts or escaped the level during these chases, while others have not been so lucky. Over time, these wanderers began to feel terrified of these chases, always afraid of encountering such a life-and-death moment again, afraid of the never-ending chase. This fear is embodied in the form of Level !, and the wanderer is forced to experience such a life-and-death moment again. |-| Sub-level of Fourteen= In fact, Level ! was once considered a sub-level of Level 14. This is largely due to the fact that the exterior of the hospital is too similar to Level 14, as well as many other similarities inside. It was once erroneously numbered Level 14.5, and to this day, many people believe that Level ! once existed as a sub-level of Level 14, but is now separated from it for some reason.



At the end of the presentation, many of the audience are leaving, and you are getting ready to leave the room. Dr. Hertz stops you. "What do you think of today's presentation? Questions? ” "Benefited a lot," you replied. After a brief pause, you ask. "However, Dr. Hertz, in today's talk, I noticed a problem. You say Level ! was destroyed in The Flicker. However, most of the levels that were destroyed in The Flicker have not been restored. Why is Level ! now reappearing? ” Dr. Hertz smiled, "Very good. You noticed, it's already much better than those stupid in suits. This is indeed a very meaningful question, and I'm sure I've just found the answer. If you want to know the answer, you can come with me. ” You couldn't hold back your curiosity, so you walked away with Dr. Hertz. You walk through one corridor after another at the base, greeted by many armed men and researchers. Finally, in a small corner at the end of the base, you arrive at Dr. Hertz's office. He pushes the door in, sits at his desk, and motions for you to sit next to him. Then he took out a typewriter and started typing. [[Level !/To Impresario B.|To Impresario B.]]


2025.6.1
Rewritten by [[User:所有盒子|Everybox]]
}} }}}} }}}}}}
